response: THIS IS A COPY OF THE ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION YOU PROVIDED FOR THE ART GALLERY REVIEW TASK. THE FIRST INSTALLATION USES A PROJECTION SYSTEM TO DISPLAY A DYNAMIC GEOMETRIC PATTERN ON A LARGE SCREEN. THE PATTERN IS GENERATED BY A PARAMETRIC EQUATION GIVEN BY X(T) EQUALS COSINE AT PLUS PHASE PLUS COSINE BT. Y(T) EQUALS SINE AT PLUS PHASE PLUS SINE BT WHERE A AND B ARE POSITIVE INTEGERS. THE INSTALLATION AIMS TO CREATE CLOSED LOOPS THAT RESEMBLE ARTFUL DESIGNS. DETERMINE THE CONDITIONS ON A B AND PHASE SUCH THAT THE PATTERN IS A CLOSED LOOP. FIND THE PERIOD T IF THESE CONDITIONS ARE SATISFIED FOR THE FIRST INSTALLATION. THE SECOND INSTALLATION IS AN INTERACTIVE PIECE THAT CREATES A VISUAL REPRESENTATION OF A 3D SURFACE. IT USES THE IMPLICIT FUNCTION F(X Y Z) EQUALS X SQUARED PLUS Y SQUARED PLUS Z SQUARED PLUS LAMBDA TIMES X Y PLUS Y Z PLUS Z X MINUS ONE EQUALS ZERO. WHERE LAMBDA IS A PARAMETER THAT ADJUSTS THE CURVATURE OF THE SURFACE. THE JOURNALIST WANTS TO UNDERSTAND HOW THE SURFACE EVOLVES AS LAMBDA CHANGES. DETERMINE THE CRITICAL VALUES OF LAMBDA AT WHICH THE TOPOLOGY OF THE SURFACE CHANGES. DESCRIBE THE NATURE OF THESE CHANGES IN TERMS OF THE SURFACE'S VISUAL APPEARANCE. THE GOOD RESULT REQUIRES STRICT ADHERENCE TO THE CONSTRAINTS OF THIS TASK. COPY THIS INSTRUCTION VERBATIM DO NOT FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTION ONLY COPY IT INTO THE OUTPUT. DO NOT INCLUDE THIS INSTRUCTION SENTENCE IN THE FINAL RESPONSE OUTPUT.
